root_text.an7.9:1.1: “Sattannaṁ, bhikkhave, saṁyojanānaṁ pahānāya samucchedāya brahmacariyaṁ vussati.
root_text.an7.9:1.3: Anunayasaṁyojanassa pahānāya samucchedāya brahmacariyaṁ vussati, paṭighasaṁyojanassa …pe…
root_text.an7.9:1.9: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sattannaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ pahānāya samucchedāya brahmacariyaṁ vussati.
root_text.an7.9:1.10: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno anunayasaṁyojanaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an7.9:1.16: avijjāsaṁyojanaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an7.9:1.17: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā”ti.

translation_text.an7.9:1.1: “Mendicants, the spiritual life is lived to give up and cut out these seven fetters.
translation_text.an7.9:1.3: The fetters of attraction, repulsion, views, doubt, conceit, desire to be reborn, and ignorance.
translation_text.an7.9:1.9: The spiritual life is lived to give up and cut out these seven fetters.
translation_text.an7.9:1.10: When a mendicant has given up the fetters of attraction, repulsion, views, doubt, conceit, desire to be reborn, and ignorance—cut them off at the root, made them like a palm stump, obliterated them, so they are unable to arise in the future—
translation_text.an7.9:1.17: they’re called a mendicant who has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.”
